title: Pure Electric 6-7 Seat SUVs in AU — Comparison
summary: 6 vehicles qualify. IONIQ 9 and EV9 lead on value, Model X is dead in AU, Model Y is smaller.
body_html:
Context

Comparing pure BEV (no PHEVs) SUVs with 6-7 seats confirmed arriving in Australia in 2026. Boris's family is accustomed to premium large-SUV levels. All prices RRP before on-road costs unless noted.

Quick Summary Table

VehiclePrice (RRP)SeatsReal-World RangeCharging (10-80%)Boot (7 up)Boot (seats down)Availability
Kia EV9$97,000–$121,0007~420–450 km~24 min333 L2,318 LNow
Hyundai IONIQ 9$105,000 (est.)6 or 7~470–500 km~24 min338 L2,419 LQ2 2026
Volvo EX90$124,990–$134,9906 or 7~480 km~30 min310 L1,915 LNow
Mercedes EQS SUV$180,000+7 opt.~500 km~31 min195 L2,100 LNow
BMW iX (6-seat)$165,900+6 opt.~500 km~35 min500 L1,750 LLimited
Tesla Model Xn/a (AU)6 or 7~480 km~27 min357 L2,577 LDiscontinued AU

Leaders on value

The IONIQ 9 and EV9 sit in a price bracket noticeably below the European options while giving up very little on range or interior space. The IONIQ 9 edges ahead on real-world range; the EV9 edges ahead on availability — it's already on the road in AU, whereas the IONIQ 9 is Q2 2026.

What's dead

Tesla Model X is effectively dead in Australia — Tesla has stopped taking orders and there's no AU-specific refresh in the 2026 pipeline. The Model Y is still sold, but it's a 5-seater with a rarely-fitted 7-seat pack that doesn't fit adults in the third row.

Recommendation

If Rachel wants a 7-seater today, the EV9 is the pragmatic choice. If you can wait until Q2, the IONIQ 9 is worth a test drive — same platform, more range, arguably nicer interior.

Want me to draft test-drive booking emails for Kia and Hyundai dealerships in Sydney?
